Independent read-only verification supports the pending production_ready determination: (1) the CP712 binding (registry.js:307) matches the baseline, contract current_pack/latest_pack, manifest, and README; (2) I recomputed the deliverable distribution from included_units and it equals implementation 23 / security_audit 19 / ui 8 / claude_review 5 / test 16 / failure_recovery 64 / hermes_evidence 10 / fixture 5 = 150, matching the prompt; (3) descriptor/case-set generation is descriptor-only with a comprehensive no-write attestation, and validators (validators.js:8) reject sensitive payloads, runtime opening, cross-tenant access, audit-body exposure, and real data while requiring human-final-approval and forbidding any Claude-final-approval or enterprise-trust claim; (4) Hermes is confined to deterministic evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false) and is not embedded in product runtime; (5) tests cover CP712 binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and contract projection (model.test.js:52-54); (6) the contract latest_projection is CP712 with validation.valid=true. The pack is honest about its state (manifest/inspection/adjudication report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status pending_review), the queue confirms CP00-712 as live latest with CP00-713 next, and CP178+ review-evidence hardening holds (readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON; no auth failure, malformed JSON, or tool-call-shaped output; no prior invalid review attempts).
